channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: MCP vs API: The protocol every developer needs to know
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a new protocol designed for connecting LLMs and AI agents to tools, data, and systems without manual hardcoding. Unlike traditional APIs, MCP enables models to autonomously discover and use tools while supporting middleware interactions with multiple services like Gmail, Notion, and Jira.

item.title: Sonnet 5 (Fully Tested): IT UNDERPERFORMS GLM-5.2 and COSTS MORE!?
item.description: AICodeKing: AICodeKing tests Claude Sonnet 5 against competing models and finds it underperforms Opus and GLM-5.2 while costing more. The review cites poor real-world coding performance, weak benchmark results, and high pricing relative to alternatives. The author concludes Sonnet 5 is overpriced and less useful than competing models.

item.title: Breaking silos with agentic AI: How Google Cloud transforms enterprise data into real-time action
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates FynanceAI, an enterprise agentic AI platform built on Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit and the open source Model Context Protocol. The system autonomously orchestrates data and executes business tasks across Oracle databases, Looker BI, and vector data stores while maintaining security and operational control.

item.title: The “Father of the Internet” is finally retiring
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Vinton Cerf, a creator of internet protocols and Google's chief internet evangelist, is retiring from his role at Google. The announcement appears in TechCrunch AI.

item.title: [AINews] Sonnet 5 today, and Fable 5 tomorrow
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space reports on the release of Sonnet 5 and upcoming Fable 5 models. The post emphasizes that the AI landscape is opening up again with new model releases.

item.title: White House drops restrictions on Anthropic AI models after two-week ban - The Washington Post
item.description: Google News AI: The White House imposed restrictions on Anthropic AI models for two weeks, then reversed course and dropped those restrictions. This represents a policy decision affecting a major AI organization and its model deployment.

item.title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
item.description: TechCrunch AI: The Trump administration has dropped restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models. Anthropic announced it would restore access to Fable beginning July 1.

item.title: Wayve launches $85M employee tender offer at $8.5B valuation
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Wayve launched an $85M employee tender offer at an $8.5B valuation. This is part of a broader trend where AI startups use employee tenders as a strategic mechanism to attract and retain talent.

item.title: San Francisco supervisors balk at 362-page, AI-assisted city code rewrite
item.description: tastyface: San Francisco city supervisors expressed concern about a 362-page city code rewrite that was generated with AI assistance. The supervisors were critical of relying on AI to draft comprehensive municipal legal documents.

item.title: US lifts curbs on Anthropic's Fable, Mythos AI models
item.description: lossolo: The US lifted regulatory restrictions on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos AI models. This removal of curbs indicates prior export or operational constraints have been eased.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1 livestream from Moscone West in San Francisco on June 30, 2026. Programming includes keynote sessions and main-stage talks focused on software factories and AI engineering topics.

item.title: WhiteHouse lifts export control on Anthropic that froze its most advanced models
item.description: dstala: The White House lifted export controls on Anthropic that had previously restricted deployment of its most advanced models. This regulatory change affects Anthropic's ability to distribute frontier AI systems.

item.title: Forward Deployed Engineers and the future of software engineering
item.description: Latent Space: Natalie Meurer from Sierra discusses the convergence of product engineers and forward deployed engineers as a trend shaping the future of software engineering. The article explores how these two roles are beginning to merge in practice.

item.title: Quoting Anthropic
item.description: Simon Willison: Anthropic announced that the US Department of Commerce lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. The company stated it would restore access to these models starting the next day and provide a further update.

item.title: Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
item.description: Pragmata: The Department of Commerce has removed export restrictions on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. This represents a shift in U.S. export policy for advanced AI systems.

item.title: Anthropic Mythos & Fable 5 export restrictions lifted
item.description: bhouston: Anthropic has lifted export restrictions on its Mythos and Fable 5 models. The models are now available for broader distribution and use.

item.title: Ahmad Osman on why local AI is catching up
item.description: Latent Space: Ahmad Osman argues that local AI capabilities are rapidly advancing across consumer, mobile, and enterprise infrastructure. The commentary follows his participation in AIEWF workshops focused on this shift.

item.title: AI Shocks Again: Google Post-AGI , New Claude, Microsoft 7 AI, 92% Human Robot, Fable 5 Backlash
item.description: AI Revolution: A YouTube news segment covering major AI developments including Google's post-AGI research, Anthropic's Claude evolution, Microsoft's seven new AI models, China's MOYA humanoid robot, and backlash to Anthropic's Fable 5 release. The segment positions these as significant industry shocks and notes emerging interest in prompt engineering as a new opportunity area.

item.title: The reason Russia and China can't win at sea - Sarah Paine
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: A video discussion between Dwarkesh Patel and Sarah Paine analyzing geopolitical and military factors affecting naval power in Russia and China. The content focuses on strategic analysis rather than artificial intelligence systems or techniques.

item.title: Jim Cramer says the AI trade has shifted — and these stocks are leading now - CNBC
item.description: Google News AI: Jim Cramer provides investment commentary on shifts in the AI stock market, identifying which companies are currently leading the sector. The analysis focuses on changing dynamics in AI-related equities and trading patterns.

item.title: Nano Banana 2 Lite
item.description: Simon Willison: Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite Image is Google's fastest and cheapest image generation model, optimized for velocity and scale. Simon Willison tested it with a Where's Waldo-style prompt and found improved results compared to earlier Nano Banana models, though with some text rendering errors.

item.title: TabFM: A zero-shot foundation model for tabular data
item.description: brandonb: TabFM is a zero-shot foundation model designed to work with tabular data. It enables inference on tabular datasets without task-specific fine-tuning.

item.title: AI vs. Dedicated Software: The Real Efficiency Debate #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A YouTube short by Cyril Imhof examines whether integrating AI for every task delivers long-term efficiency gains or represents a costly trap. The video questions whether dedicated software solutions still outperform general AI approaches despite current enthusiasm.

item.title: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
item.description: TechCrunch AI: OpenClaw, a free open source agentic program, is now available on Android and iOS platforms. The tool extends access to agentic capabilities to mobile devices.

item.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
item.description: MIT Technology Review AI: Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new flagship product designed to support scientific research. Similar to Claude Code for software engineering, Claude Science can autonomously execute meaningful work from high-level instructions and includes specialized capabilities for scientific tasks.

item.title: What's new in Claude Sonnet 5
item.description: Simon Willison: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5, matching Opus 4.8 performance at lower cost. The model includes a new tokenizer that increases token counts by approximately 30% relative to Sonnet 4.6. Key changes include a 1 million token context window, 128,000 max output tokens, adaptive thinking enabled by default, and removal of temperature/top_p/top_k sampling parameters.

item.title: The One Job AI Can't Replace, According to @3blue1brown
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel's YouTube interview with 3Blue1Brown discussing which job categories are resistant to AI replacement. The segment focuses on identifying roles where AI may have inherent limitations in capability or application.

item.title: Claude Code Just Got 5x More Expensive
item.description: vincent_s: Claude Code pricing increased by 5x according to a HackerNews post. The post title signals commentary on cost changes for Anthropic's Claude Code product.

item.title: Local Reasoning for Global Properties
item.description: mpweiher: Local reasoning for global properties is an approach to verify or reason about system-wide properties by examining local components or behavior. This technique enables scalable reasoning about complex systems by decomposing global verification into local checks.

item.title: Show HN: Morph Reflexes – Multi-head classifiers for agent traces
item.description: bhaktatejas922: Morph Reflexes is an API-first system for extracting semantic signals from agent traces using multi-head classifiers built on a custom vLLM-forked inference engine. It reuses prefill compute across multiple classification heads with sub-30ms inference latency and less than 2ms overhead per additional reflex, enabling cost-effective monitoring of agent behaviors like looping and reasoning leakage at scale.

item.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
item.description: TechCrunch AI: EquiLibre Technologies, founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is a Prague-based AI lab now valued at over $500 million. The team previously built poker AI and now applies their expertise to quantitative hedge fund trading.

item.title: Anthropic Just Released Claude Sonnet 5 and it's VERY IMPRESSIVE!
item.description: Cole Medin: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5, which the creator characterizes as an excellent model for its cost. The video discusses the model's capabilities and presents a positive assessment of its performance relative to pricing.

item.title: Germany Heatwave MELTED my Ice Cream in Lisbon! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A YouTube short documents extreme heat affecting Germany and Lisbon, with ice cream melting as a visible symptom. The video attributes the intensity to urban heat island effects in concrete-heavy cities and frames it within broader climate change discourse.

item.title: Sonnet 5 is LIVE And It Competes With Opus
item.description: Chase AI: Claude Sonnet 5 is a new release from Anthropic that represents a significant upgrade to the Sonnet series. The video reviews benchmarks showing Sonnet 5 performs close to Claude Opus 4.8, and discusses whether these benchmarks translate to real-world task performance.

item.title: Build your first Android app in AI Studio in 5 minutes
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: A tutorial demonstrating how to build a native Android photography app in five minutes using Google AI Studio and Gemini 3.5 Flash. The video covers initial prompting, app prototyping, sideloading to devices, and debugging including photo orientation fixes.

item.title: Tell HN: Installing Cursor on iOS irreversibly changes your privacy settings
item.description: zkldi: A Cursor iOS app user reports that logging into the app automatically switched their privacy setting from "Privacy Mode (Legacy)" (no code storage) to the newer "Privacy Mode" (allows code storage for background agents). The deprecated setting became inaccessible afterward and support confirmed it cannot be reverted within the app. The user criticizes this as unclear and potentially unlawful.

item.title: ScarfBench: Benchmarking AI Agents for Enterprise Java Framework Migration
item.description: Hugging Face: Hugging Face introduced ScarfBench, a benchmarking tool for evaluating AI agents on enterprise Java framework migration tasks. This enables standardized measurement of AI agent performance in real-world software engineering scenarios.

item.title: Claude Sonnet 5
item.description: marinesebastian: Claude Sonnet 5 is a large language model announced or discussed in the Hacker News community. The post title suggests news or commentary about this model release.

item.title: AI Learns, Takes Initiative, Works Asynchronously: The Future is Here! #shorts
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof presents a perspective on AI systems that learn, take initiative, and operate asynchronously in ambient mode. The piece frames these capabilities as demonstrating organizational learning and represents a near-future vision of AI autonomy.

item.title: The AI Compass
item.description: Simon Willison: The AI Compass is a 29-question political compass-style quiz that categorizes respondents into one of 30 AI philosophy archetypes based on their views on AI ethics and development. It is implemented as a single-page React application using inline Babel transpilation to avoid build steps.

item.title: Designing GPU-Accelerated Query Engines with NVIDIA GQE
item.description: NVIDIA Developer: NVIDIA GQE is a framework for designing GPU-accelerated query engines that leverages NVIDIA hardware advances including high bandwidth memory to overcome memory and I/O bandwidth constraints in query processing.

item.title: How to build a continuous evaluation pipeline for multi-agent systems with Gemini
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to build a continuous evaluation pipeline for multi-agent AI systems using Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform and Cloud Run. The pipeline implements data-driven assessment with adaptive rubrics, shadow deployments, and CI/CD automation to detect regressions in agent quality.

item.title: Why is AI expensive all of a sudden?
item.description: Alberta Tech: Video explores why AI has become expensive, covering token pricing economics and the trend of "tokenmaxxing" where developers optimize code to consume more tokens. Discusses the real underlying reasons for rising AI costs with reference to industry analysis.

item.title: AI: The ROI Runway Could Be Long Outside the Tech Sector
item.description: mfiguiere: The article discusses AI implementation timelines and return on investment (ROI) expectations, suggesting that non-tech sectors may face longer periods before realizing measurable returns on AI investments compared to technology companies.

item.title: He Built $100B at Google, Then Failed. Now He Runs an $80B+ Company | Snowflake, Sridhar Ramaswamy
item.description: EO: Sridhar Ramaswamy, CEO of Snowflake, discusses his career arc from building Google's ad business to founding Neeva (a failed search engine startup) and now leading Snowflake's transition into the AI era. He shares lessons on leadership principles, hiring adaptable talent, staying close to product, and resilience after failure.

item.title: Claude Science
item.description: lebovic: Claude Science is a tool or service offering from Anthropic. The exact capabilities and purpose are not detailed in the available information.

item.title: Introducing Claude Science (now in beta)
item.description: Claude: Claude Science is a new research application from Anthropic in public beta that runs analyses, searches scientific databases, and provides reproducible artifacts with full code and environment tracking. It is pre-configured for genomics, single-cell analysis, proteomics, structural biology, and cheminformatics, and integrates with 60+ scientific databases.

item.title: Expanding our Heat Resilience data to 50+ global cities
item.description: Google Research: Google Research has expanded its Heat Resilience data service to cover 50 or more global cities. This initiative supports climate and sustainability applications by providing data to help cities understand and mitigate heat-related risks.

item.title: Have your agent record video demos of its work with shot-scraper video
item.description: Simon Willison: shot-scraper 1.10 introduces a video command that accepts a storyboard.yml file to define automated routines against web applications and uses Playwright to record video demonstrations. This enables coding agents to automatically produce visual demos of their work by exercising web features through recorded browser interactions.

item.title: SkillOpt: Agent skills as trainable parameters
item.description: Microsoft Research: SkillOpt is a technique that treats agent skills as trainable parameters, converting manual skill editing into a systematic training process. This approach improves agent reliability without modifying underlying model weights.
